mnemonics: G.I.G.O. Prune: Gini/Info Gain guides growth, Overfitting demands Pruning.
keyPoints: Decision Trees are built using algorithms like ID3 (Iterative Dichotomiser 3) and CART (Classification and Regression Trees), which use metrics like Information Gain or Gini Impurity to select the best feature to split on.
Validation is critical and is done using techniques like a hold-out test set or cross-validation to estimate the model's performance on unseen data before pruning.
Pruning (like Reduced Error Pruning or Cost Complexity Pruning) is performed to remove non-essential branches to reduce overfitting and create a simpler, more generalizable tree.
commonMistakes: Using the training data to evaluate the model, which gives an overly optimistic accuracy and does not reveal overfitting.
Pruning too much or too little; aggressive pruning can cause underfitting, while no pruning leads to overfitting.
Forgetting to set a minimum sample size for a leaf node, which can create leaves based on very few samples and noise.
